title: Pam Malone, Bovill's sheriff
description: What Pat Malone was like, as Bovill's sheriff.

title: Medicine; Contests for a popular girl; the Great Kamama's healing linament
description: Medicine show tricks: contests for a popular girl; scissors that cut glass; the Great Kamama's healing linament; new gadgets for suckers.

title: Entertainment; literary debater subjects
description: Entertainment, such as house sweats. Literary debate subjects.

title: Courtship methods; husband expected to make a living; the woman is to work at home
description: Courtship methods. Old forms of entertainment compared with new. A newly married couple sets up house. The husband expected to make a living, the woman to work at home.

title: Farmwork done by exchange; owning a stump ranch
description: Farmwork done by exchange. Owning a stump ranch.

title: Bovill compared to other backwoods towns; violence
description: Bovill not much compared to other backwoods towns. Little serious violence.

title: High moral standards among loggers
description: T. P. Jones insists on high moral standards among the loggers.

title: How a moonshiner could be successful; still camouflages as a cedar tree
description: Jacks didn't drink on the job. How a moonshiner could be successful, and not get caught. A still camouflaged as a cedar tree.

title: Gambler pulls a gun; gambling accepted by management
description: A gambler pulls a gun. Gambling is accepted by management.

title: Foreigners in logging camps; buying war bonds
description: Foreigners in the logging camps. Many speak Slavonian, a "universal" language. They won't buy war bonds, but will gladly give a man money. They disappear after we get into the war. They're friendly, open people.

title: Bad conditions in early bunkhouses; getting rid of "greybacks"
description: Bad conditions in early bunkhouses. The stench. A way to get rid of "greybacks" (body lice).

title: IWW role in improving conditions; 4-L delegate; camp goes on strike
description: IWW role in improving conditions. They try to persuade Hershiel to join. Their unreasonable talk at Spokane meeting, which Hershiel attends as a 4-L delegate. The 4-L's want to bring both sides together. Hershiel's camp goes on strike. The strikers disappear. Most IWW's are foreigners.

title: Early railroad strike
description: An early railroad strike - greenhorns worked.

title: Loggers hired from Spokane
description: Loggers hired from Spokane. Hershiel has trouble clerking for 30 new men. One logger lives like a king in Spokane.

title: Salary costs to Potlatch during strike; men deputized to carry guns
description: Salary costs to Potlatch during strike. Men deputized to carry guns; why Hershiel stopped carrying his.

title: High regard for managemnet
description: Hershiel had high regard for management. T. P. Jones tries to hold Hershiel's job for him.

title: Tricks of scaling; log measuring; cheating on scales; methods of scaling defective logs
description: Tricks of scaling. Scalers protected by inspections. Variations in log measuring. Hershiel protects woods scalers. He doesn't offend a friend by telling him his log is a cull. He has a complainer scale for himself. T. P. Jones said, "If there's a doubt, give it to the workingman." Cheating on scales leads to more work for scaler. Methods of scaling defective logs. Separate records for various species and lengths.

title: Al species were logged; costs of relogging an area; clearcutting vs. leave trees
description: All species were logged. Limbing was a chore. Prohibitive costs of relogging an area. Clearcutting vs. leave trees.
